---
title: "draft1"
format: html
---

```{r}
# R packages
library(tidyverse)
library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)

# Data
library(ipumsr)
ddi <- read_ipums_ddi("data/ahtus_00001.xml")
df <- read_ipums_micro(ddi)
```

### Data Cleaning

```{r}
filtered_data <- df %>%
  filter(
    CIVSTAT == 1,
    HHTYPE == 1,
    UNDER18 >= 1,
    AGE >= 22 & AGE <= 40,
    EDUC %in% c(5, 6),
    AGEYNGST >= 0 & AGEYNGST <= 18,
    SEX %in% c(1, 2)
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    sex_label = ifelse(SEX == 1, "Male", "Female"),
    ACT_UNDOM_HRS = ACT_UNDOM / 60
  )
```

### Initial Visualization 1

```{r}
# Paid, Unpaid, and Childcare Work by Sex (2018)

summary_data <- filtered_data %>%
  mutate(
    sex_label = ifelse(SEX == 1, "Male", "Female"),
    # Convert minutes to hours
    childcare = ACT_CHCARE / 60,
    unpaid    = ACT_UNDOM / 60,
    paid      = ACT_WORK / 60
  ) %>%

# Reshape
pivot_longer(
  cols = c(childcare, unpaid, paid),
  names_to = "work_type",
  values_to = "hours"
) %>%

# Group for averages
group_by(sex_label, work_type) %>%
summarise(
  hours = mean(hours, na.rm = TRUE),
  .groups = "drop"
)

# Set stacking order for the bars, first level is the bottom of the bar
# Desired order: childcare (bottom), unpaid (middle), paid (top)
summary_data$work_type <- factor(
  summary_data$work_type,
  levels = c("childcare", "unpaid", "paid")  # <-- CHANGE ORDER HERE
)

ggplot(summary_data, aes(x = sex_label, y = hours, fill = work_type)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity") +
  labs(
    title = "Paid, Unpaid, and Childcare Work by Sex (2018)",
    x = "Sex",
    y = "Average Hours per Day",
    fill = "Work Type"
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  scale_fill_manual(
    values = c(
      "childcare" = "red",     # bottom
      "unpaid" = "orange",       # middle
      "paid" = "gray"          # top
    ),
    labels = c(
      "childcare" = "Childcare",
      "unpaid" = "Unpaid Domestic Work",
      "paid" = "Paid Work"
    )
  )
```

### Initial Visualization 2

```{r}
# Unpaid Domestic Work by Sex (1930-2018)

# Average hours by year and sex
summary_data <- filtered_data %>%
  group_by(YEAR, sex_label) %>%
  summarise(avg_domestic_hours = mean(ACT_UNDOM_HRS, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop")

# Line plot
ggplot(summary_data, aes(x = YEAR, y = avg_domestic_hours, color = sex_label)) +
  geom_line(size = 1) +
  geom_point() +
  labs(
    title = "Unpaid Domestic Work by Sex (1930 - 2018)",
    x = "Year",
    y = "Average Hours per Day",
    color = "Sex"
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("Male" = "steelblue", "Female" = "salmon"))

# Note for 2018
# 2.72 hrs for female
# 1.57 hrs for male
```

```{r}
#| eval: false

# Print all column names
sapply(df, function(x) attr(x, "label"))
```

### Clustering (2D)

```{r}
# Select time-use variables
time_vars <- c(
  "ACT_WORK", "ACT_CHCARE", "ACT_EDUCA",
  "ACT_INHOME", "ACT_MEDIA", "ACT_OUTHOME",
  "ACT_PHYSICAL", "ACT_TRAVEL", "ACT_PCARE",
  "ACT_UNDOM", "ACT_CIVIC"
)

# Keep only selected variables
df_time <- df[, time_vars]

# Drop missing values
df_time <- na.omit(df_time)

# Sample data (raw dataset is too large)
set.seed(123)
df_sample <- df_time[sample(nrow(df_time), 5000), ]

# Standardize variables
df_scaled <- scale(df_sample)

# Run PCA
pca_res <- prcomp(df_scaled, center = TRUE, scale. = TRUE)

# Optional scree plot (to determine how many PCs to use)
eig_vals <- pca_res$sdev^2
var_explained <- eig_vals / sum(eig_vals)

plot(var_explained, type = "b",
     xlab = "Principal Component",
     ylab = "Proportion of Variance Explained",
     main = "Scree Plot (PCA)",
     pch = 16)

# Use the first 2 PCs
pca_scores <- pca_res$x[, 1:4]

# Distance matrix on PCA scores
dist_mat <- dist(pca_scores)

# Hierarchical clustering (Ward's method)
hc <- hclust(dist_mat, method = "ward.D2")

# Dendrogram
plot(hc, labels = FALSE, main = "Hierarchical Clustering")

# Cut into clusters
k <- 2
clusters <- cutree(hc, k = k)

# Cluster counts
table(clusters)

# Plot clusters in PCA space
plot(pca_scores[,1], pca_scores[,2],
     col = clusters,
     pch = 16,
     xlab = "Principal Component 1",
     ylab = "Principal Component 2",
     main = "Hierarchical Clusters in PCA Space")

# Add legend
legend("topright",
       legend = paste("Cluster", sort(unique(clusters))),
       col = sort(unique(clusters)),
       pch = 16,
       bty = "n")
```

```{r}
# Employment mapped onto clusters

time_vars <- c(
  "ACT_WORK", "ACT_CHCARE", "ACT_EDUCA",
  "ACT_INHOME", "ACT_MEDIA", "ACT_OUTHOME",
  "ACT_PHYSICAL", "ACT_TRAVEL", "ACT_PCARE",
  "ACT_UNDOM", "ACT_CIVIC"
)

# Keep only selected variables + employement status
df_time <- df[, c(time_vars, "NOEMPLOY")]

# Drop missing values
df_time <- na.omit(df_time)

# Sample data (needed for clustering stability)
set.seed(123)
df_sample <- df_time[sample(nrow(df_time), 5000), ]

# Create employment label
df_sample$empstat <- ifelse(df_sample$NOEMPLOY == 0, "Unemployed",
                    ifelse(df_sample$NOEMPLOY == 1, "Employed",
                           "Other"))

# Standardize time-use variables
df_scaled <- scale(df_sample[, time_vars])

# Run PCA
pca_res <- prcomp(df_scaled, center = TRUE, scale. = TRUE)

# View the principal components
pca_res$rotation[, 1:4]

# Extract first 4 PCs
pca_scores <- as.data.frame(pca_res$x[, 1:4])

# Hierarchical clustering on PCA space
dist_mat <- dist(pca_scores)
hc <- hclust(dist_mat, method = "ward.D2")

# Cut into clusters
k <- 4
clusters <- cutree(hc, k = k)

# Attach cluster + employement status
pca_scores$cluster <- factor(clusters)
pca_scores$empstat <- df_sample$empstat

# -------------------------
# Plot 1: Employement Status only
# -------------------------
plot(pca_scores$PC1, pca_scores$PC2,
     col = ifelse(pca_scores$empstat == "Unemployed", "blue", "red"),
     pch = 16,
     xlab = "Principal Component 1",
     ylab = "Principal Component 2",
     main = "PCA Space Colored by Employment")

legend("topright",
       legend = c("Unemployed", "Employed"),
       col = c("blue", "red"),
       pch = 16,
       bty = "n")

# -------------------------
# Plot 2: Clusters + Employement Status
# -------------------------
plot(pca_scores$PC1, pca_scores$PC2,
     col = pca_scores$cluster,
     pch = ifelse(pca_scores$empstat == "Unemployed", 16, 17),
     xlab = "Principal Component 1",
     ylab = "Principal Component 2",
     main = "Clusters + Employment Status in PCA Space")

legend("topright",
       legend = c("Cluster 1","Cluster 2","Cluster 3","Cluster 4"),
       col = c(1:4, "black","black"),
       pch = c(rep(16,4),16,17),
       bty = "n")

# -------------------------
# Optional: cluster-by-empstat table
# -------------------------
table(pca_scores$cluster, pca_scores$empstat)
```

### Clustering (3D)

```{r}
# Load packages
library(plotly)

# Select time-use variables
time_vars <- c(
  "ACT_WORK", "ACT_CHCARE", "ACT_EDUCA",
  "ACT_INHOME", "ACT_MEDIA", "ACT_OUTHOME",
  "ACT_PHYSICAL", "ACT_TRAVEL", "ACT_PCARE",
  "ACT_UNDOM", "ACT_CIVIC"
)

# Keep complete cases
df_time <- na.omit(df[, time_vars])

# Standardize data
df_scaled <- scale(df_time)

# Run PCA
pca_res <- prcomp(df_scaled, center = TRUE, scale. = TRUE)

# Extract first 4 principal components
scores <- as.data.frame(pca_res$x[, 1:4])

# Run clustering (optional, if you want colors)
set.seed(123)
clusters <- kmeans(scores, centers = 4, nstart = 25)$cluster
scores$cluster <- factor(clusters)

# 3D PCA plot
plot_ly(
  scores,
  x = ~PC1,
  y = ~PC2,
  z = ~PC3,
  color = ~cluster,
  type = "scatter3d",
  mode = "markers"
)
```

```{r}
# Load package
library(plotly)

# -------------------------
# Variables
# -------------------------
time_vars <- c(
  "ACT_WORK", "ACT_CHCARE", "ACT_EDUCA",
  "ACT_INHOME", "ACT_MEDIA", "ACT_OUTHOME",
  "ACT_PHYSICAL", "ACT_TRAVEL", "ACT_PCARE",
  "ACT_UNDOM", "ACT_CIVIC"
)

# Keep data + sex, drop missing
df_time <- na.omit(df[, c(time_vars, "SEX")])

# -------------------------
# Gender label
# -------------------------
df_time$gender <- ifelse(df_time$SEX == 1, "Male",
                   ifelse(df_time$SEX == 2, "Female", "Non-Binary"))

# -------------------------
# Standardize
# -------------------------
df_scaled <- scale(df_time[, time_vars])

# -------------------------
# PCA (3 components)
# -------------------------
pca_res <- prcomp(df_scaled, center = TRUE, scale. = TRUE)

scores <- as.data.frame(pca_res$x[, 1:3])

# -------------------------
# Clustering (k-means)
# -------------------------
set.seed(123)
clusters <- kmeans(scores, centers = 4, nstart = 25)$cluster
scores$cluster <- factor(clusters)

# Add gender
scores$gender <- df_time$gender

# -------------------------
# 3D PCA plot (colored by sex)
# -------------------------
plot_ly(
  scores,
  x = ~PC1,
  y = ~PC2,
  z = ~PC3,
  color = ~gender,
  colors = c("blue", "red", "gray"),
  type = "scatter3d",
  mode = "markers"
) %>%
  layout(
    title = "3D PCA Space Colored by Sex",
    scene = list(
      xaxis = list(title = "PC1"),
      yaxis = list(title = "PC2"),
      zaxis = list(title = "PC3")
    )
  )
```
